_comment: Metadata required for initial Sankey render. Two top-level keys: nodes (node metadata) and intervention (company placements).

nodes.description: Passenger transport for holiday, visiting family, shopping, sport; associated material production (steel, aluminum, plastics) and manufacturing of cars.
Passenger transport for work, business and education; associated material production (steel, aluminum, plastics) and manufacturing of cars.
Freight transport fuel use; material production (steel, aluminum, plastics) and manufacturing of trucks and ships.
Hot water detergents, cosmetics and pharmaceuticals, incl. some packaging; energy use in washing machines and dishwashers; manufacturing of washing machines and dishwashers.
Heated and cooled space.
Energy used by light devices.
Energy in use and manufacturing of electronics; writing and printing paper; energy use in computing infrastructure.
Textile industry energy use; production of polymer fibres; fertilizer for cotton (energy and N2O emissions)
Production of some steel and aluminum; energy use by the industrial machines production sector.

Production of steel, aluminum and chemicals for construction and furniture uses; cement production; energy use in construction and quarrying industries; energy use in the wood industry incl. land-use change emissions; emissions from vegetation clearing for settlements.

Energy use for cooking; energy cost of fertilizer production (part of the chemical industry); energy use in the food
processing industry; energy use in chemical, aluminum and paper industries associated with food and drink
packaging; energy use on farms (tractors, irrigation systems); N2O emissions from fertilizer use; CH4 from rice,
livestock and manure management; land-use change for agriculture.
methane emissions from waste and wastewater.
Transport by cars, 2- and 3- wheeled motorised vehicles and buses. Transport by trucks, vans, and ships. Remainder of transport by planes and trains.
Transport by trucks, vans, and ships. Remainder of transport by planes and trains.
As defined by IEA34: emissions from fuels, electricity and heat consumed by households, excluding transport and including households with employed persons.

As defined by IEA34: emissions from fuels, electricity and heat consumed by the following sectors: Wholesale and retail trade; Warehousing and support activities for transportation; Postal and courier activities; Accommodation and food service activities; Information and communication; Financial and insurance activities; Real estate activities; Professional, scientific and technical activities; Administrative and support service activities; Public administration (excluding military); Education; Human health and social work activities; Arts, entertainment and recreation; Repair and installation of machinery, equipment and transport vehicles; Water supply; sewerage, waste management and remediation activities. After UN ISIC Rev.435
Includes emissions from energy use in Facilities from all industries defined below.
Emissions from Manufacture of basic iron and steel and Casting of iron and steel (as defined in UN ISIC Rev.435), excluding facilities.

Emissions from Manufacture of chemicals and chemical products (incl. fertilizer) and Manufacture of basic pharmaceutical products and pharmaceutical preparations (as defined in UN ISIC Rev.435), excluding facilities.
Emissions from Manufacture of other non-metallic mineral products (cement, lime, glass) (as defined in UN ISIC Rev.435), excluding facilities.
Emissions from Manufacture of basic precious and other non-ferrous metals and Casting of non-ferrous metals (as defined in UN ISIC Rev.435), excluding facilities.
Emissions from Manufacture of food products, beverages, and tobacco products (as defined in UN ISIC Rev.435), excluding facilities.
Emissions from Manufacture of paper and paper products and Printing and reproduction of recorded media (as defined in UN ISIC Rev.435), excluding facilities.
Emissions from Manufacture of textiles, wearing apparel and leather and related products (as defined in UN ISIC Rev.435), excluding facilities.
Manufacturing industry not included above, in the order of significance: Machinery, Mining and quarrying, Transport equipment, Construction and Wood industry.

Emissions from land-use change, soil disturbance, fertilizer use and on-farm energy use (including tractors) associated with production of livestock feeds and livestock rearing itself. Does not include food processing, transport, cooking or emissions from fertilizer production.

Emissions from land-use change, fertilizer use and on-farm energy use (including tractors) associated with production of vegetal agricultural products. Does not include food processing, transport, cooking or emissions from fertilizer production.

Emissions from land-use change, fertilizer use and on-farm energy use (including tractors) associated with production of vegetal agricultural products intended for human consumption directly. Does not include food processing, transport, cooking or emissions from fertilizer production.
Emissions form land-fills and (not treated or inappropriately treated) waste water.
Cars and 2- and 3- wheeled motorised vehicle use.
Bus and Minibus use.
Truck and Van use.
Ship use (domestic and international navigation)
Aviation (domestic and international aviation)
Rail.
Computing infrastructure energy usage for data centers, cryptocurrency and AI
Conveyor belts, Pumps, Refrigerator (industrial use), air compressor and other motorised systems used in Industry.
Refrigerator (used in households and commercial), cooker, washer, dryer, dishwasher, electronic devices
Cooling (air conditioning) of residential, commercial, public and industrial buildings.
Illumination of residential, commercial, public and industrial buildings. Excludes illumination by transport devices.
Water heating for washing.
Heating of residential, commercial, public and industrial buildings.
High, medium and low temperature application: blast furnace, arc furnace, smelter, oven.
Medium and low temperature application involving steam: petrochemical cracker, reaction vessel, cleaning facility (after 9).
Diesel use in agriculture sector, assumed to be mostly associated with Tractor use on farm.
Emissions from non-energy uses of fossil fuels, primarily as feedstocks for petrochemicals and chemical production.
Emissions from industrial gases used in refrigeration, air-conditioning, and other chemical processes.
Emissions generated during the production of cement.
Land-use change emissions associated with timber and pulp production.
Land-use change emissions associated with creating space for settlements and infrastructure.

Non-energy emissions from land-use change, soil disturbance and fertilizer use associated with production of vegetal agricultural products for human consumption. Includes methane emissions from Rice paddies.
Non-energy emissions from land-use change, soil disturbance, and fertiliser use during the cultivation of crops grown for fibre (e.g. cotton, jute, flax).
Non-energy emissions from land-use change, soil disturbance and fertilizer use associated with production of grains and other feedlots fed to livestock.
Non-energy emissions from land-use change, soil disturbance and manure use associated with pasture land-use.
Emissions resulting from enteric fermentation in livestock digestive systems and manure not applied to any land.
Emissions from decomposing waste material buried in land disposal sites.
Emissions from sewage and industrial water when it is untreated or inadequately treated.
Spark ignition Otto engine: car, bus, truck, generator, garden machinery (incl. two-stroke)
Compression ignition diesel engine in trucks, cars, buses, ships, trains, generators
Turbofan and turbo propeller engines.
Steam (coal/biomass) or natural gas powered engine.
Aviation (domestic and international aviation)
Data centers, crytocurrencies, and AI.
Computers, televisions, portable devices
AC/DC induction motor (excl. Refrigeration)
Light devices excluding light devices in vehicles.
Electric resistance heater, electric arc furnace.
Direct heat application, heat from CHP, district heat, heat pumps.
Oil combustion device: boiler, petrochemical cracker, chemical reactor
Gas combustion device: open fire, stove, boiler, chemical reactor
Coal combustion device: open-fire, stove, boiler, blast furnace, chemical reactor
Wood/biomass combustion device: open fire, stove, boiler.
Emissions from non-energy uses of fossil fuels, primarily as feedstocks for petrochemicals and chemical production.
Emissions from industrial gases used in refrigeration, air-conditioning, and other chemical processes.
Emissions generated during the production of cement.
Land-use change emissions associated with timber and pulp production.
Land-use change emissions associated with creating space for settlements and infrastructure.
Land-use change associated with making space for annual and permanent crops (incl. shifting agriculture, but deducing re-growth effects from it; excluding crops used for biofuels)
N2O emissions from fertilizer use from soil and indirect (leached nitrogen)
Methane emissions from anaerobic conditions characteristic for paddy rice agriculture.
Emissions from manure (applied or not-applied).
Non-energy emissions enteric fermentation of ruminants (cattle, sheep, goats).
Emissions form land-fills and (not treated or inappropriately treated) waste water.
Direct oil use - correspond to direct emissions in other GHG data-sets.
Energy use and transformation losses in non-electricity energy industries: refineries, mines, oil and gas platforms, coke ovens, blast furnaces gas works, liquefactions plants, BKB plants.
Fugitive natural gas from oil extraction (flared or vented), oil and gas transportation and coal mines. Includes fossil fuels fires.
Electricity and heat production, including auto producers and including own use of electricity.
Direct natural gas use - correspond to direct emissions in other GHG data-sets.
Direct coal and peat use - correspond to direct emissions in other GHG data-sets.
Land-use change associated with making space for crops grown for production into liquid fuels (includes bioethanol and biodiesel). Includes emissions from fertilizer application.
Land-use change emissions associated with de-forestation and forest degradation from dry biomass collection.
Emissions from industrial gases used in refrigeration, air-conditioning, and other chemical processes.
Emissions generated during the production of cement.
Emissions from cutting down and removing forests for timber or land use.
Emissions from land cleared to build cities, towns, or infrastructure.
Emissions released when land is cleared for farming activities.
Emissions that arise when fertilisers are applied to fields.
Emissions produced during flooded rice cultivation.
Emissions from storing and managing animal waste.
Emissions released during digestion by ruminant animals.
Emissions from burning solid waste in incineration facilities.
Emissions form land-fills and (not treated or inappropriately treated) waste water.
Emissions from using oil and refined petroleum fuels for energy.
Emissions from burning natural gas for energy.
Emissions from combusting coal for energy.
Biomass processing emissions.
Emissions from industrial gases used in refrigeration, air-conditioning, and other chemical processes.
Process emissions released when limestone (calcium carbonate) is heated in cement production, breaking down into lime and releasing CO₂
Emissions from converting forests, grasslands, or other ecosystems to new land uses.
Emissions released when soil microbes process nitrogen from fertilisers and organic matter.
Emissions formed by microbes breaking down organic material in low-oxygen environments.
Carbon dioxide emissions
Methane emissions
Nitrous oxide emissions
Fluorinated gas emissions

companies.par1: Fervo Energy is helping expand access to next-generation geothermal power systems.
companies.par2: Within the emissions model, Fervo's technology is most active in the electricity and heat pathway, where lower-carbon baseload energy can help reduce dependence on fossil fuel generation across multiple sectors of the economy.
